intro: Hi! I'm the AI Upscaler support assistant. I answer from every support topic we've ever handled (each with a step-by-step guide), and I can search all GitHub issues live. Ask about install errors, GPU not used, Docker/NAS setup, API tokens, models, and more.

entries.title: Plugin won't install / checksum mismatch
entries.answer:
Jellyfin checks the plugin ZIP's MD5 against the repo manifest and rejects any mismatch. A checksum error on a current version almost always means a **stale cached repo**. Step by step:
1. Use the official repo URL: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kuschel-code/JellyfinUpscalerPlugin/main/repository-jellyfin.json`
2. Dashboard → Plugins → **Repositories** → remove the AI Upscaler repo.
3. Re-add the repository (same URL).
4. **Restart Jellyfin** (this clears the cached manifest).
5. Catalog → AI Upscaler → install the latest (**v1.7.8**).
6. Still failing? Paste the exact error + your Jellyfin version.

entries.title: "No newer version available" / can't update
entries.answer:
Jellyfin caches the plugin catalog. Step by step:
1. Dashboard → Plugins → **Repositories**.
2. Remove the AI Upscaler repository.
3. Re-add it (URL: `.../main/repository-jellyfin.json`).
4. **Restart Jellyfin**.
5. Hard-refresh the dashboard (Ctrl+F5).
6. Catalog now shows the latest (**v1.7.8**).

entries.title: Plugin "Not Supported" / incompatible with my Jellyfin
entries.answer:
The plugin targets Jellyfin ABI **10.11.8**. Step by step:
1. Check your Jellyfin version (Dashboard, bottom of the page).
2. On Jellyfin **10.11.x** → install plugin **v1.7.8**.
3. On older Jellyfin → update Jellyfin first, then install.
4. Restart Jellyfin after installing.
5. Still "Not Supported"? Check the server log for the ABI line and match the versions.

entries.title: Unable to connect to the Docker AI service
entries.answer:
The plugin reaches the Docker container over HTTP. Step by step:
1. Confirm the container is **running**.
2. Confirm port **5000** is published (`-p 5000:5000`).
3. In a browser open `http://YOUR-SERVER-IP:5000/health` → expect `{"status":"healthy"}`.
4. In the plugin set **AI Service URL** = `http://YOUR-SERVER-IP:5000` (use the LAN IP, not `localhost`, if Jellyfin runs in its own container).
5. Make sure no firewall / reverse proxy blocks port 5000.
6. Click **Test Connection** in the plugin config.

entries.title: GPU detected but inference runs on CPU (slow)
entries.answer:
If `/gpu-verify` shows `AzureExecutionProvider`, you're on the wrong/old image (plain onnxruntime, no GPU provider). Step by step:
1. Choose the right image: `docker7-intel` / `docker7-amd` / `docker7` (NVIDIA).
2. Force a fresh pull:
```
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d --force-recreate
```
3. Pass the GPU device: `--gpus all` (NVIDIA), `/dev/dri` (Intel/AMD Linux), or `/dev/dxg` (WSL2).
4. On native Linux also add `group_add: render`.
5. Re-check `http://IP:5000/gpu-verify` → `active_providers` should list a non-CPU provider.
(v1.7.8 also fixed a display bug where `using_gpu:false` showed even with OpenVINO active — trust `active_providers`.)

entries.title: Intel Arc / iGPU on Windows or WSL2 not used
entries.answer:
Step by step for Intel Arc / iGPU on Windows/WSL2:
1. Use the **`docker7-intel`** image (it bundles `onnxruntime-openvino`).
2. Pass **`/dev/dxg`** (NOT `/dev/dri/renderD128`) and mount `/usr/lib/wsl`.
3. Force a fresh image: `docker compose pull` (plain `up` reuses the cached old one).
4. `docker compose up -d --force-recreate`.
5. Check `http://IP:5000/gpu-verify` → should show `OpenVINOExecutionProvider`.
6. For full-length files use **batch** mode — live 4x HD is too heavy for an A380.

entries.title: ONNX error / "Reshape" / wrong input type / FP16 crash
entries.answer:
Both ONNX warmup errors are fixed. Step by step:
1. Update to the **v1.7.8** Docker images: `docker compose pull && docker compose up -d --force-recreate`.
2. The FP16-on-FP32 crash (`INVALID_ARGUMENT`) was fixed in v1.7.4 — FP16 is only used when the loaded model agrees.
3. The `Reshape` warmup error on `realesrgan-x4-256` was fixed in v1.7.8 — the benchmark now reads the model's real input shape.
4. Restart the container and retry the model.

entries.title: "Api token not configured" message
entries.answer:
The Docker AI service refuses requests without a token. **Home/LAN (simplest)** — step by step:
1. Add to the container environment: `API_TOKEN=disable`.
2. Restart the container.
3. The message is gone; model downloads work.

**To secure it instead** — step by step:
1. Set `API_TOKEN=your-secret` on the container.
2. Enter the **same** secret in the plugin's **AI Service Token** field.
3. Save in the plugin, then restart the container.

entries.title: Non-admin users can't use the plugin (models won't load)
entries.answer:
Fixed in **v1.7.5** (31 endpoints downgraded from admin-only to authenticated-user). Step by step:
1. Update the plugin to **v1.7.8**.
2. Restart Jellyfin.
3. Have the non-admin user reload the player — they can now load/use models.

entries.title: 4:3 video is stretched to fill the screen
entries.answer:
Fixed in **v1.7.5** (`object-fit: contain` on the real-time overlay). Step by step:
1. Update the plugin to **v1.7.8**.
2. Restart Jellyfin.
3. Reload the video player — 4:3 sources now letterbox correctly instead of stretching.

entries.title: AMD GPU support (ROCm / Vulkan)
entries.answer:
Two AMD paths. **ROCm (`docker7-amd`)** — step by step:
1. Use the `docker7-amd` image (v1.7.8 fixed it actually using the GPU; earlier it ran on CPU).
2. Pass `/dev/kfd` **and** `/dev/dri`, plus `--group-add video`.
3. Check `/gpu-verify` → `ROCMExecutionProvider`.

**Vulkan (`docker7-vulkan`, best for older cards)** — step by step:
1. Use the `docker7-vulkan` image.
2. Pass `/dev/dri`.
3. ncnn-Vulkan runs on most AMD/Intel GPUs.

entries.title: NVIDIA / RTX error, crash, or provider load failure
entries.answer:
Step by step for NVIDIA:
1. Use the **`docker7`** (NVIDIA) image.
2. Install the NVIDIA Container Toolkit on the host.
3. Run with `--gpus all` (or `runtime: nvidia` in compose).
4. Verify GPU passthrough: `docker exec <container> nvidia-smi` must succeed.
5. If you saw `INVALID_ARGUMENT` or a `libonnxruntime_providers` load error, update to **v1.7.8** (FP16 + provider fixes) and re-pull.
6. Check `/gpu-verify` → `CUDAExecutionProvider` / `TensorrtExecutionProvider`.

entries.title: Real-time keeps falling back to WebGL / poor playback
entries.answer:
Real-time server upscaling drops to WebGL when the GPU can't sustain the FPS — by design. Step by step for real quality:
1. Don't rely on live 4x HD (too heavy for most GPUs, even an Arc A380).
2. Use the **scheduled task** (batch) to pre-process the file once.
3. Play the resulting `<name>_upscaled` file normally.
4. For live playback, keep it light: a fast model (`realesr-general-x4v3`) and/or 2x scale.

entries.title: Batch upscale job stuck at 95% / where are the files?
entries.answer:
95% is the final **re-mux** stage. Step by step to diagnose:
1. The finished file is `<name>_upscaled.<ext>` next to the source; a failed partial is auto-deleted (so nothing's left to find).
2. Intermediate frames live in the container's `/tmp` (not host-visible).
3. While it's stuck, run `docker logs --tail 80 <container>` and `docker exec <container> df -h /tmp`.
4. If `/tmp` is ~100% full, add a volume in compose: `- ai-tmp:/tmp`.
5. Recreate the container and re-run the job.

entries.title: Which model should I use?
entries.answer:
Pick by goal. Step by step:
1. **Fast / low-VRAM:** `realesr-general-x4v3` (~5MB), `lsdir-compact-v2-x4`, `spanx2-ch48`.
2. **Best quality:** `nomos2-dat2-x4`, `drct-l-x4`, `nomos8k-hat-l-x4` (heavy — poster/backdrop).
3. **Compressed / streaming sources:** `realwebphoto-v4-dat2-x4`.
4. **Anime:** `real-cugan-x4`, `apisr-anime-x2`.
5. Download it from the `http://IP:5000` dashboard, then select it in the player overlay or set `DEFAULT_MODEL`.

entries.title: Setup on ZimaOS / CasaOS / Synology / TrueNAS / Unraid
entries.answer:
Deploy with docker compose. Step by step:
1. In your NAS app manager, add a custom app / import a Docker Compose:
```
services:
  ai-upscaler:
    image: kuscheltier/jellyfin-ai-upscaler:docker7-cpu
    ports: ["5000:5000"]
    environment: ["API_TOKEN=disable"]
    volumes: ["ai-models:/app/models"]
    restart: unless-stopped
volumes:
  ai-models:
```
2. Intel iGPU? Use `docker7-intel` and add `devices: ["/dev/dri:/dev/dri"]`.
3. Start it, open `http://NAS-IP:5000` to confirm it's up.
4. In the plugin set **AI Service URL** = `http://NAS-IP:5000`.
5. Download a model. (TrueNAS: a ready compose is in `docker-ai-service/truenas/`.)

entries.title: Scan only specific libraries (not everything)
entries.answer:
Implemented in v1.6.1.14+. Step by step:
1. Update to **v1.7.8**.
2. Dashboard → Plugins → AI Upscaler → **Settings** tab.
3. In the **library picker**, tick the libraries you want scanned.
4. Save. (Leave all unticked = scan everything, the default.)

entries.title: Old docker5 / docker6 tag missing
entries.answer:
The old tag families (`docker4/5/6/6.1`) are retired. Step by step:
1. In your compose/run, change the image tag to **`docker7-<backend>`** (e.g. `docker7-intel`), or pin **`:v1.7.8-<backend>`**.
2. `docker compose pull`.
3. `docker compose up -d`.

entries.title: Getting started / is the plugin usable?
entries.answer:
From zero. Step by step:
1. Deploy the **Docker AI service** (compose — see the NAS/setup topic).
2. Open `http://server:5000` → confirm the dashboard loads and **download a model**.
3. In Jellyfin, install the plugin from the repo.
4. Plugin config → **AI Service URL** = `http://server:5000` → Save.
5. Use it: **batch** mode (nightly scheduled task) upscales your library; **real-time** mode enhances during playback.
6. See the Installation and Configuration pages for details.

entries.title: Plugin won't enable / "Failed to load assembly" / disappears (tombstoned)
entries.answer:
Almost always **missing transitive DLLs** (`dotnet build` emits only the main DLL). Step by step:
1. Install the **official release ZIP** — it contains all required DLLs. Don't hand-copy a single DLL.
2. If you built from source, use `dotnet publish -c Release` (NOT `dotnet build`) and ship the full publish output.
3. Confirm the plugin folder has: `JellyfinUpscalerPlugin.dll`, `FFMpegCore.dll`, `CliWrap.dll`, `Instances.dll`, `SixLabors.ImageSharp.dll`, `meta.json`.
4. Re-enable via REST: `POST /Plugins/{id}/{version}/Enable` with an admin `X-Emby-Token`.
5. Restart Jellyfin; ensure the version matches ABI 10.11.8 (v1.7.8).

entries.title: Plugin settings won't save / changes don't apply
entries.answer:
An old bug where saving dropped fields (Docker URL/IP/port reset to defaults), **fixed in v1.6.1.14**. Step by step:
1. Update the plugin to **v1.7.8**.
2. Restart Jellyfin.
3. Dashboard → Plugins → AI Upscaler → re-enter your settings.
4. Click **Save**.
5. If a field still resets on the latest version, open an issue naming that field.

entries.title: No Settings / config page after installing
entries.answer:
Step by step:
1. The config lives at **Dashboard → Plugins → AI Upscaler** (click the plugin name).
2. No entry there? The plugin didn't load — see the "Plugin won't enable" topic (install the official ZIP, restart Jellyfin).
3. Update to **v1.7.8** and restart Jellyfin.
4. Hard-refresh the browser (Ctrl+F5).

entries.title: FFmpeg/ffprobe crash — "Cannot start process / file name not provided"
keywords: file name has not been provided
entries.answer:
An old ffmpeg cold-start bug (paths captured before Jellyfin resolved them), **fixed in v1.6.1.21**. Step by step:
1. Update to **v1.7.8**.
2. Dashboard → Playback → confirm Jellyfin's bundled FFmpeg is detected.
3. Restart Jellyfin.
4. Re-run the scheduled task — paths are now late-resolved on every entry point.

entries.title: Run the Setup Doctor (one-shot self-diagnosis)
entries.answer:
v1.7.9 adds a one-shot Setup Doctor that inspects YOUR running AI service and tells you exactly what to fix. Step by step:
1. Open http://YOUR-SERVER-IP:5000/doctor in a browser (or run: curl http://IP:5000/doctor | jq). The Docker dashboard also has a 'Setup Check' button on the Hardware tab.
2. Read the rows top to bottom - each is green (ok) / amber (warn) / red (fail) with a copy-paste fix.
3. backend: which image/GPU was detected (info only).
4. gpu_provider_active: red means you are on CPU - pull the matching docker7-<nvidia|amd|intel> image and pass the GPU device.
5. device_passthrough: red prints the exact line for your backend (--gpus all, or /dev/dri, or WSL2 /dev/dxg + group_add).
6. onnx_provider_pkg: red means the wrong onnxruntime build (a plain onnxruntime shadows the GPU one) - pull the clean docker7-<backend> image.
7. api_token: warn if no token is set - use API_TOKEN=disable for a trusted LAN, or the same token on both the plugin and the service.
8. model_smoke: runs a tiny inference on the loaded model; 'no model loaded yet' is fine on a fresh start, not an error.
Paste the /doctor output into a GitHub issue and we can pinpoint the problem in one reply.

entries.title: Anime4K mode (anime shader) - how to use it / it does nothing
entries.answer:
Anime4K is a real-time WebGL anime SHADER (Anime4K 4.0.1) - not a neural net, and it needs no server. As of v1.7.9 it actually runs; older builds loaded a dead CDN and silently fell back to plain sharpening. Step by step:
1. Update the plugin to v1.7.9 or newer.
2. In a WEB client (browser, not a native app) start an anime title.
3. Plugin settings -> Realtime Mode -> 'Anime4K (anime shader)' (or switch it in the in-player quick-menu), then play.
4. Confirm in the browser console (F12): success shows 'AI Upscaler RT: Anime4K (embedded, SIMPLE_M 2x) started'. In the Network tab, 'configurationpage?name=UPSCALERAnime4K' should return 200.
5. If your client lacks WebGL2 float textures, the console shows 'Anime4K unsupported ... using Lanczos' and it auto-falls back to the sharpen shader (no error, just not anime upscaling).
Notes: it runs client-side, so no Docker/GPU server is needed; it's best on anime/cartoon content. For live-action or guaranteed maximum quality, use Server AI or the batch scheduled task instead.
